issues.title: spring-ai-starter-model-chat-memory-jdbc auto configures the deprecated `JdbcChatMemory`
issues.body: `spring-ai-starter-model-chat-memory-jdbc` auto configures the deprecated `JdbcChatMemory`. it should, one imagines, auto configure a `MessageWindowChatMemory` with a `JdbcChatMemoryRepository`

comments.body:
It configures a deprecated JdbcChatMemory since we have the M8 release cycle where it is deprecated before removal in RC1.

`MessageWindowChatMemory` is configured in `spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-memory`

Use the starter `spring-ai-starter-model-chat-memory-jdbc` to get both.

issues.title: Remove `spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-qianfan` module
issues.body:
The `auto-configurations/spring-ai-autoconfigure-qianfan` [module](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/tree/main/auto-configurations/models/spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-qianfan) remains with an empty [pom.xml](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/auto-configurations/models/spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-qianfan/pom.xml) despite not being officially declared and listed in the [parent POM](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/pom.xml#L32-L208) as a proper Maven module.

This was even true as of the `1.0.0-M8` release (see [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/v1.0.0-M8/auto-configurations/models/spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-qianfan/pom.xml)).

comments.body:
thanks.  There were a few stragglers that I was in the middle of moving and must have got distracted.

They now reside in the [community module](https://github.com/spring-ai-community/qianfan/commit/5390b5fb8042e88a7b97d7cb344534e766a205ee).

There was still a reference in our BOM!  

now the only references to qianfan are in *.adoc.

issues.title: please make the SQL tables that are generated - in at least the JdbcChatMemory and the PgVectorStore implementations - consistent.
issues.body:
please make the SQL tables that are generated - in at least the JdbcChatMemory and the PgVectorStore implementations - consistent. 

`AI_CHAT_MEMORY`
`SPRING_AI_VECTORS `

Maybe `SPRING_AI_CHAT_MEMORY` as a useful default?

comments.body: moreover the schema needs to be customizable.

Yah. Customization is good. The schema for the existing supported databases aren’t easily overridden. Schema for unsupported databases however are easy to provide: just add a .sql file in the class the sql initializer would look for it `/src/man/resources/org/springframework/ai/blah/schema-oracle.sql` 

The initialiser substitutes the platform name in the file name. That already ‘just works’ 

I don’t know (I’m afk) if there’s a way to tell our various JDBC-backed things to back off for the databases we do support. I’d be surprised if we didn’t. Don’t we have initialize-schema=true or something ?

> Don’t we have initialize-schema=true or something ?

FYI, Spring Boot use enum `DatabaseInitializationMode` instead of `boolean`, and default to `EMBEDDED`, I'm trying to create PR to align with Boot.

> > Don’t we have initialize-schema=true or something ?
> 
> FYI, Spring Boot use enum `DatabaseInitializationMode` instead of `boolean`, and default to `EMBEDDED`, I'm trying to create PR to align with Boot.

Created https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3010.
closing as this PR https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3055 address the issue and we have a few issues on this topic.
